canPickMany.description: Add checkboxes to enable selection of multiple values.

showName.description: Show the names of the parameter in front of the selected value in the status bar.

showSelection.description: Show the selected value of the parameter in the status bar.

initialSelection.description: Preselect a value (or values) the first time the parameter loads, before any selection has been stored. Reference each value exactly as it is identified among the values to pick from: a plain value by its string; a value with a display label by its `value` (the returned token, NOT the shown label); a named-output value by its `displayValue` label (it has no single string). Use a string for a single-select parameter, or an array of strings when `canPickMany` is true (a lone string still works and preselects one value). Examples: "release" (plain), "-O2 -DNDEBUG" (the `value` of a labelled entry, not its "Release" label), "GCC" (the `displayValue` of a named entry), ["debug", "release"] (multi-select).

joinSeparator.description: The separator used to join multiple selected values when the parameter is substituted into a task (only relevant with canPickMany=true). Backslash escapes (\n, \t, \r, \\) are interpreted. Defaults to a single space.
